title: Bothriocline smithii
taxonomy: Bothriocline smithii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Bothriocline, which is a group of plants commonly known as the bluebells or blue lobelias. Bothriocline smithii is native to the tropical regions of East Africa. The plant's classification is further supported by its botanical name, which is a combination of the genus and species names. intro: Bothriocline smithii, also known as Smith's Begonia or Bothriocline, is a plant species belonging to the family Lamiaceae, characterized by its small, fragrant flowers and medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which may help alleviate digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions.
